response.justification: The candidate substantially exceeds the experience bar with roughly 15+ years of fullstack work and directly matches the core stack: TypeScript, Node.js, and React in recent roles (Optilyz, re:cinq), MongoDB/MySQL, AWS with CDK, Docker, and Kubernetes. Notably, his current role includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which satisfies the unusual 'AI agent development' requirement that most applicants will lack. His testing pedigree is exceptional (TDD practice, authored test frameworks, taught testing courses). Gaps are moderate: no explicit Nest.js or Vitest, no Kotlin or Python, and much of his frontend depth is in Ember rather than React, so React seniority and Nest.js ramp-up should be probed in an interview.
strengths: Recent, relevant TypeScript/Node.js/React/AWS/MongoDB production experience plus hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work
Deep testing and software-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, taught Selenium/testing courses)
Breadth across backend, frontend, mobile, and infra (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab CI) with 15+ years of experience and a CS degree
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience, the two named framework specifics of the primary stack
Kotlin and Python are absent from the skill set, and Java exposure appears dated and limited
Significant portions of career depth are in niche technologies (DLang, Ember.js), so modern React depth needs verification
